1.02.01.title: Document Control
sections: Establish control of all food safety documents and records so that only current, approved versions are in use at Goodness Gardens facilities.
All FSQA documents: policies, SOPs, work instructions, forms, logs, and external standards, at all GG sites.
The FSQA Manager is the document control owner: approves, issues, and retires documents.
Department leads ensure only current versions are posted or used at stations.
All employees use only controlled copies; uncontrolled printouts are for reference only.
Each document carries a Document Control Number (PrimusGFS-aligned numbering), revision, date revised, and author/revisor initials in the header block.
New or revised documents are drafted per 1.03.01, reviewed by the FSQA Manager, and approved before issue.
The master document register (this system + the Drive master folder) lists every controlled document, its number, type, and revision.
Obsolete versions are removed from points of use; one historical copy is archived and marked OBSOLETE.
Documents are reviewed at least annually, and after any process change, audit finding, or regulatory change.
Records are completed in ink or electronically, legible, signed and dated, never altered with correction fluid — corrections are single-line strikethrough with initials.
Records are retained for a minimum of 24 months or as required by regulation/customer, whichever is longer.
Master document register; archived obsolete documents; annual review log.

1.03.01.title: SOP Creation and Control
sections: Define how SOPs are created, formatted, approved, distributed, and revised.
All standard operating procedures and work instructions at all GG sites.
Department managers draft or request SOPs for their operations.
SOPs follow the GG template: header block (logo, title, document control number, date revised, author initials) plus Objective, Scope, Responsibility, Procedure, and Records sections.
Document numbers mirror the PrimusGFS question they support (e.g., 5.09.02 Glass & Brittle).
Draft SOPs are routed to the FSQA Manager for technical review and to the affected department for practicality review.
On approval the SOP is issued per 1.02.01 and affected employees are trained on it before it takes effect; training is documented.
Revisions increment the revision number and are summarized in the revision history.
Approved SOPs; revision history; SOP training sign-in sheets.

1.03.03.title: Corrective Action and Non-Conformance Procedure
sections: Establish a corrective and preventive action (CAPA) system so every non-conformance is investigated to root cause and corrected to prevent recurrence.
All non-conformances: internal/external audit findings, deviations, customer complaints, pest activity, environmental positives, recalls, and regulatory findings.
FSQA Manager owns the CAPA log and verifies effectiveness.
Department managers implement corrective actions by the assigned due dates.
Record each non-conformance on a CAPA Report (1.03.03-01R) and enter it in the CAPA Log (1.03.03-02R) with a unique number.
Take immediate correction first: contain, hold, and segregate any affected product per 5.03.
Perform root cause analysis (5 Whys / contributing factors); 'employee error' alone is not an acceptable root cause.
Define corrective action(s) with an owner and due date; preventive action where the cause could affect other lines or sites.
FSQA verifies implementation and, after an appropriate interval, verifies effectiveness before closing.
CAPA status is reviewed monthly; overdue items are escalated to site management.
In this system: deviations auto-open a CAPA assigned to the FSQA Manager, and the incident cannot close until the RCA is complete and all actions are closed.
CAPA Reports; CAPA Log; effectiveness verification records.

1.03.03-01R.title: CAPA Report Template
sections: Single-page report used to document one non-conformance and its corrective action through closure.
Open within 24 hours of identifying the non-conformance.
Attach supporting evidence (photos, lab results, logs).
Cross-reference the CAPA Log entry number.

1.03.03-02R.title: CAPA Log
sections: Running log of all CAPAs for trending and audit review.
Reviewed monthly by FSQA; trends (repeat causes, repeat areas) feed management review.

1.04-01R.title: Internal Audit Record
sections: Record of an internal audit event: scope, findings, and follow-up.
File with the completed checklist; raise a CAPA for every non-conformance.

1.04.01.title: Internal Audits
sections: Verify through scheduled internal audits that the food safety system is implemented and effective.
All GG facilities, all PrimusGFS modules applicable to each site.
FSQA Manager maintains the audit schedule and qualifies auditors.
Auditors must be trained and independent of the area audited where practical.
Monthly GMP internal audits are performed at each facility using checklist 1.04.01R-IA-GMP (organic addendum where applicable).
A full-system self-audit against all applicable PrimusGFS modules is completed at least annually — use the Primus Self-Audit module in this system, which scores Compliant / Non-Compliant / N/A.
Every non-conformance automatically opens a deviation with CAPA per 1.03.03.
Audit results are reported to site management and trended in management review.
Follow-up audits verify closure of prior findings.
Audit schedule; completed checklists and self-audit submissions; CAPA log entries.

1.04.01R-IA-GMP.title: Monthly GMP Internal Audit Checklist
sections: Monthly GMP internal audit of the facility.
Personnel hygiene: hairnets/beard nets worn, clean clothing, no jewelry, no eating/drinking outside designated areas
Handwash stations stocked (soap, towels, warm water) and in use; signage posted
Production floors, drains, walls, ceilings clean and in good repair; no condensation over product
No flaking paint, rust, loose metal, exposed wood, or temporary repairs in product zones
Glass & brittle plastic register current; items intact (spot check 10)
Chemicals labeled, stored locked, away from product; SDS available; mixing records current
Pest devices in place per map, intact, serviced; no activity observed
Storage areas: product off floor, 18-inch wall clearance, FIFO followed, packaging protected
Doors/dock seals close properly; no daylight gaps; outside areas free of harborage/standing water
Pre-op, sanitation, ATP, and CCP records complete and signed for the period
Non-conforming product identified, segregated, and on the hold log
Trash removed on schedule; waste areas clean; lids in place
Employee training records current for new hires in the period
Maintenance work orders closed out food-safe (no tools/parts left, area released by QA)
Score each item C / NC / N/A with comments and photos; raise CAPA for each NC; auditee and FSQA sign.

1.04.01R-IA-GMP-ORG.title: GMP + Organic Internal Audit Checklist
sections: Monthly GMP internal audit addendum for organic handling (NOP compliance).
Organic product identified and segregated from conventional at receiving, storage, production, and shipping
No prohibited substances used on organic lines; sanitizers on the approved organic list with rinse/verification as required
Organic integrity maintained: dedicated or cleaned-and-purged equipment with records
Labels and lot codes preserve organic identity through traceability
Organic certificates for suppliers current on file
Pest control in organic areas uses approved methods only
Complete together with 1.04.01R-IA-GMP at facilities handling organic product.

1.04.02.title: Regulatory Inspections
sections: Ensure regulatory inspections (FDA, USDA, state, county) are handled consistently and professionally.
All GG facilities and all regulatory visits, announced or unannounced.
Site manager and FSQA Manager (or designee) host all inspections.
Receptionist/first contact verifies credentials and notifies the host immediately.
Verify the inspector's credentials and record name, agency, and purpose.
Notify the FSQA Manager immediately; the inspector is always accompanied.
Provide records on request; duplicate anything provided and log what was shared.
Take parallel photos/samples whenever the inspector does.
Do not sign statements admitting violations without management review; corrections of obvious issues should begin immediately.
Hold a closeout meeting, document all observations, and open CAPAs for any findings per 1.03.03.
FSMA inspections: traceability records (FSMA 204) must be retrievable within 24 hours.
Inspection log; copies of anything provided; CAPA entries; agency reports.

1.04.04-01NW.title: Net Weight Assurance
sections: Assure packed product meets declared net weight requirements.
All packed product at GG packing facilities.
QA/line leads perform checks; FSQA verifies scale calibration program.
Scales are calibrated per schedule with certified weights; calibration is recorded and tags applied.
At start-up and at defined intervals, sample packages are checked against declared net weight (tare excluded).
Underweights stop the line: adjust fill, re-check, and rework affected product.
Results are recorded; repeated failures open a CAPA.
Net weight check records; scale calibration records.

1.06-01.title: Supplier Approval
sections: Approve and monitor suppliers of product, packaging, and food-contact materials.
All suppliers of raw product, seed, packaging, processing aids, and chemicals.
FSQA Manager approves suppliers and maintains the approved supplier list.
Purchasing buys only from approved suppliers.
New suppliers provide: third-party GFSI/PrimusGFS audit certificate (or GG questionnaire + risk assessment), guarantee/continuing letter, and specs.
Suppliers are risk-ranked; high-risk suppliers require annual document renewal and periodic performance review.
Incoming goods inspection (5.13.01) verifies condition and documentation each receipt; failures are recorded against the supplier.
Repeated non-conformances trigger corrective action requests or removal from the approved list.
FSVP requirements apply to imported items per 1.08 FSVP documents.
Approved supplier list; supplier files (certs, specs, guarantees); supplier non-conformance records.

1.06.02.1R.title: Contract Service Providers Policy and Lists
sections: Policy and current list of approved contract service providers.
Provider name and service (pest control, sanitation, calibration, laboratory, waste, uniforms)
Verify licenses annually; labs must be ISO 17025 accredited for required tests.

1.07-CMR-01-CFF.title: Customer Feedback Form
sections: Capture and investigate customer feedback and complaints.
Complaint category (quality, foreign material, illness allegation, packaging, service)
Food safety complaints (foreign material, illness) are escalated to the FSQA Manager same day and assessed for recall per 1.07-RT-01.

1.07-RT-01.title: Recall and Trace
sections: Enable rapid and complete recall or withdrawal of affected product.
All product packed or shipped by GG, all sites.
The Recall Team is led by the FSQA Manager with operations, sales, and management members named in the Recall Plan.
Traceability: every lot carries a traceability lot code (TLC) linking seed/source, production date, line, and customers shipped (FSMA 204 KDEs per 1.08.01-FSMA204).
On a potential recall trigger (complaint, test result, supplier notice, regulator), the Recall Team convenes within 2 hours and classifies the event (Class I/II/III or withdrawal).
Affected lots are identified, quantified (Recall Inventory Log 1.07-TRR-01-RIL), and customers notified using the Recall Communication Letter (RCL) and Recall Information Form (RIF).
Product on hand is held and segregated; disposition is documented.
Effectiveness checks confirm recovery percentage; regulators (FDA RES) are notified as required.
A mock recall is performed at least annually targeting >99% recovery within 2 hours, documented on the Mock Recall Cover Form (MR) and Product Recall Log (PRL).
Post-recall, root cause and CAPA per 1.03.03.
Mock recall records; recall file (notifications, logs, disposition, effectiveness checks).

1.07-TR-01-PRL.title: Product Recall Log
sections: Log of all recall and mock recall events.
One line per event; keep with the recall file.

1.07-TRR-01-MED.title: Media Request
sections: Script and log for media/external inquiries during a recall.
Only the designated spokesperson responds; all others refer inquiries and log them here.

1.07-TRR-01-MR.title: Mock Recall Cover Form
sections: Cover form summarizing a mock recall exercise.

1.07-TRR-01-RCL.title: Recall Communication Letter
sections: Template letter notifying customers of a recall/withdrawal.
Reason for recall and hazard statement
Send by phone + written confirmation; log all notifications.

1.07-TRR-01-RIF.title: Recall Information Form
sections: Internal form consolidating all facts of the recall event.
Maintained by the Recall Team lead as the single source of truth during the event.

1.07-TRR-01-RIL.title: Recall Inventory Log
sections: Inventory reconciliation of affected lots during a recall.
Update continuously until reconciliation reaches target.

1.07-TRR-01-SQ.title: Status Questionnaire
sections: Customer status questionnaire during recall effectiveness checks.
Completed for each notified customer; supports effectiveness percentage.

1.07.08-REPNY.title: Emergency Preparedness Mock Drill Record (NY)
sections: Record of the annual emergency preparedness mock drill (NY).
Scenario (fire, ammonia leak, power loss, flood, security)
Run at least annually per the Emergency Preparedness plan; coordinate with GG-SAF-009 incident system for any real events.

1.08-02-FFA.title: Food Fraud Assessment
sections: Assess and mitigate food fraud (economically motivated adulteration) vulnerabilities.
All purchased product, packaging, and inputs.
FSQA Manager completes and reviews the assessment annually.
Each input is scored for fraud likelihood (history, supply chain complexity, price pressure) and detectability.
High-vulnerability inputs get mitigation: trusted/approved suppliers, certificates of analysis, organic certificate verification, mass-balance checks.
The assessment is reviewed annually and on supplier or market changes.
Food fraud vulnerability assessment; mitigation records.

1.08-01R-FDA.title: Food Defense Assessment
sections: Assess food defense vulnerabilities and define mitigation at each site.
All GG facilities, exterior grounds, and transport.
FSQA Manager + site manager complete the assessment; all employees follow security rules.
Site assessment covers: perimeter/exterior, access points, visitor controls, chemical storage, water systems, open product areas, shipping/receiving.
Mitigations: locked entries with coded access, visitor log and escort, sealed trailers, chemical lockup, employee awareness training (see GG-TRN-201 food defense section).
Suspicious activity is reported immediately and investigated; see Food Defense Challenge records.
Assessment reviewed annually and after any security event.
Assessment; challenge exercise records (1.08.01 SA/NY); visitor logs.

1.08.01-SA.title: Food Defense Challenge (SA)
sections: Record of the annual food defense challenge exercise (SA site).
Date and scenario (e.g., unauthorized entry attempt)
Run unannounced annually; verifies the Food Defense plan works in practice.

1.08.01-RNY.title: Food Defense Challenge (NY)
sections: Record of the annual food defense challenge exercise (NY site).
Response observed (entry blocked, sign-in enforced, escort provided)

1.08.01-FSMA204.title: FSMA 204 Implementation and Procedure
sections: Implement FSMA 204 traceability for covered foods (leafy greens/herbs) across greenhouse and packinghouse.
All Food Traceability List items grown, packed, or shipped by GG.
FSQA Director maintains the plan; department leads capture KDEs in real time.
Critical Tracking Events (CTEs) captured: harvesting, cooling, initial packing, receiving, transformation, shipping.
Key Data Elements (KDEs) recorded at each CTE: traceability lot code (TLC), dates, quantities/UOM, location identifiers, reference documents.
TLC assigned at initial packing links seed lot, harvest date, line, and operator (see 2.6.01-R workbook).
Records are electronic where possible and retrievable within 24 hours in a sortable spreadsheet for FDA.
Mock traceability exercises validate the system with the annual mock recall.
Traceability workbook (2.6.01-R); shipping/receiving logs; mock recall records.

1.08.01-RE.title: FSMA / Food Safety Acronym Glossary
sections: Glossary of FSMA / food safety acronyms used in GG programs.
FSMA, FDA, FSQA, GFS, CTE, KDE, TLC, PCQI, CAPA, ATP, GMP, GAP, HACCP, SOP, SDS, FIFO, NOP, FSVP — each with plain-language definition and where it is used
Issued as a reference with onboarding and FSQA training; bilingual quick-ref at 2.6.01-REF.

1.08.01-RT-01.title: Recall and Trace (Rev)
sections: Current revision of the Recall and Trace plan (2026) — see 1.07-RT-01 for the full procedure.
Recall Team per the Recall Plan roster.
This revision adds FSMA 204 TLC-based tracing, updated team roster, and customer notification matrix.

2.0.title: Farm Food Safety, Hygiene and Site Management
sections: Establish farm-level food safety, hygiene, and site management for GG growing operations.
All GG greenhouse and field growing locations.
Greenhouse/Farm manager implements; FSQA verifies through audits.
Each location has a farm food safety plan with a site map (water sources, buildings, chemical storage, toilets, neighboring land risks).
Workers receive hygiene training at hire and annually (GG-TRN-203 GAP/Produce Safety).
Toilets and handwash stations stocked and serviced; located per regulation; no field defecation.
Sick workers (vomiting, diarrhea, jaundice, infected wounds) are excluded from product contact.
Animals excluded from growing areas; intrusion monitored and documented; no harvest within contaminated zones.
Adjacent land risks assessed annually; buffer zones where needed.
Visitors follow hygiene rules and sign in.
Farm food safety plan; training records; toilet service records; intrusion logs.

2.6.01-R.title: FSMA 204 Traceability — All Phases
sections: FSMA 204 traceability workbook covering all phases (seed → harvest → pack → ship).
One row per lot per CTE; retrievable within 24 hours; reconciles with shipping logs.

2.6.01-REF.title: FSMA Acronym Quick Reference (Bilingual)
sections: Bilingual quick reference of FSMA / food safety acronyms for farm staff.
Same terms as 1.08.01-RE in English and Spanish on one laminated card
Posted in break areas and issued at training.

2.7-FD-01.title: Food Defense and Food Fraud (Farm)
sections: Food defense and food fraud controls at the farm/greenhouse level.
All growing locations and farm inputs.
Greenhouses and chemical/fertilizer storage are locked outside working hours.
Water sources (wells, tanks) secured and inspected.
Inputs purchased only from approved suppliers; deliveries verified against orders (fraud control).
Unfamiliar persons challenged and reported; visitors escorted.
Annual review with the site food defense assessment (1.08-01R).
Inspection records; visitor logs; annual review.

2.07.title: Field Harvest Hygiene and Sanitation
sections: Hygiene and sanitation requirements during field/greenhouse harvest.
All harvest crews and harvest equipment.
Harvest crew leads enforce daily; FSQA audits in season.
Handwashing before harvest, after breaks/restroom; gloves where required and changed when contaminated.
Harvest tools (knives, scissors) cleaned and sanitized at defined frequency; damaged tools removed.
Harvest containers food-grade, cleaned per schedule, never placed directly on soil; bottom-contact surfaces controlled.
No eating, drinking (except water in designated areas), tobacco, or glass in harvest areas.
Product with animal contamination, decomposition, or chemical contact is not harvested — flag and report.
Harvested product shaded and moved to cooling promptly.
Sanitation logs for tools/containers; crew training records.

2.08.title: Agronomic Input Policy
sections: Control agronomic inputs (fertilizers, soil amendments, crop protection) for food safety and organic integrity.
All inputs applied to GG crops.
Grower/greenhouse manager maintains input records; FSQA approves materials.
Only approved inputs are used; organic operations use OMRI/NOP-allowed materials with documentation.
No raw manure on covered crops; composted amendments require process verification certificates.
Applications recorded: date, material, lot, rate, area, applicator; pre-harvest intervals observed.
Pesticide applications only by/under licensed applicators with WPS-compliant training, PPE, and posting (see GG-SAF-006).
Storage: segregated, labeled, locked, with secondary containment.
Input application logs (Master Growing Inputs workbook); approvals; applicator licenses.

2.09-4.09.title: Agricultural Water Management
sections: Manage agricultural water (production and postharvest) to protect produce safety.
All water contacting crops, harvest tools, or hands at growing operations.
FSQA sets testing schedule; site managers maintain systems.
Water system description and map maintained for each site (source, distribution, treatment).
Production water (hydroponic/irrigation/misting): annual system inspection + microbiological testing per schedule (5.16.01-W); generic E. coli criteria applied per FSMA Subpart E.
Postharvest/wash water: potable source, sanitizer maintained and recorded (chlorine/PAA per label), strength checked at start-up and intervals.
Failed tests: stop use, assess product impact, correct (shock/treat/repair), retest before return to service — document as a deviation with CAPA.
Backflow prevention on hose connections; hoses off the floor.
Water system maps; test results; sanitizer logs; corrective actions.

3.05.01.title: Sanitation SOP (Bilingual)
sections: Sanitation procedures for greenhouse growing operations (bilingual SOP).
All greenhouse structures, benches, trays, tools, and irrigation systems.
Greenhouse manager schedules; trained crew performs; FSQA verifies.
Master sanitation schedule (3.05.01-GH) defines what, how, frequency, chemical, and responsibility for every item.
Between crops: remove debris and media, clean surfaces, then sanitize benches/trays/floors with approved sanitizer at labeled strength.
Irrigation/hydroponic systems flushed and sanitized per schedule to control biofilm and algae.
Sanitizer strength verified with test strips and recorded (5.04.09 record).
Completed tasks signed and dated on the master sheet.
Master sanitation sheets; sanitizer mixing/strength records.

3.05.01-GH.title: Greenhouse Master Sanitation Sheets
sections: Greenhouse master sanitation schedule and sign-off workbook.
Post in each greenhouse; FSQA verifies monthly during the GMP audit.

3.06.02.title: Seeding and Planting Logs
sections: Seeding and planting logs for traceability and crop management.
One line per seeding; links seed lot to TLC per FSMA 204 (1.08.01-FSMA204).

4.05.title: Greenhouse Harvest Practices
sections: Harvest practices for greenhouse crops (basil, herbs, microgreens).
Pre-harvest assessment: water results current, no animal intrusion, no chemical PHI conflicts, crew hygiene verified.
Tools sanitized at start and at defined intervals; containers food-grade and labeled with TLC info.
Ready-to-eat rules apply: bare-hand contact minimized per posted crop rules; gloves where required (GG-TRN-205).
Harvested product moved to cooling within the defined window; temperatures recorded.
Damaged/diseased product culled — never mixed into food lots.
Pre-harvest assessments; harvest logs; cooling records.

5.01-PO-01.title: Pre-Op ATP
sections: Pre-operational inspection with ATP verification before production starts.
All food-contact equipment and production areas at packing facilities, every production day.
Sanitation completes cleaning; QA performs pre-op; production may not start until release.
After sanitation, QA visually inspects all food-contact surfaces, ceilings/overheads, and floors in the production zone.
ATP swabs are taken on rotating food-contact sites per the swab schedule; results must meet the pass limit (5.14.12).
Failures: re-clean, re-swab; record both results; investigate repeats with CAPA.
Pre-op release is signed before start-up; record retained (5.13.08 checklist).

5.01.01.title: Chemicals Control
sections: Control of cleaning and sanitation chemicals (Rev. 4).
All chemicals used in GG facilities.
FSQA approves chemicals; sanitation lead controls storage and issue.
Only approved, food-facility-appropriate chemicals are used; the approved chemical list is current with SDS for each.
Chemicals stored locked, segregated from product/packaging, labeled in secondary containers, with spill containment.
Mixing per label and recorded (5.04.09 / 5.07.01 records) with concentration verification.
Letters of guarantee on file; organic-approved list maintained where applicable.
Approved chemical list; SDS; mixing records; inventory records.

5.02.05.title: Pest Control Program
sections: Pest control program for all facilities.
Interior and exterior of all GG buildings.
Licensed contract provider services devices; FSQA reviews reports and trends; everyone reports sightings.
Device map current: exterior bait stations, interior mechanical traps and ILTs — no rodenticide inside food areas.
Service frequency: at least monthly interior/exterior (weekly during pressure); each visit documented with findings and materials.
Sightings reported immediately, logged, and corrective action documented.
Trend review quarterly; structural/door issues fed to maintenance.
Provider licensure and insurance current per 1.06.02.1R.
Service reports; device map; sighting log; trend reviews.

5.03.title: Non-Conforming Product
sections: Identify, segregate, and disposition non-conforming product.
All product that fails specification or food safety requirements.
Anyone can place product on hold; only QA dispositions.
Non-conforming product is labeled HOLD with date/reason and moved to the designated hold area.
Hold log entry created; quantity and lots recorded.
QA dispositions: release (with justification), rework, regrade, donate (if safe), or destroy — recorded with signature.
Food-safety holds require FSQA Manager disposition and CAPA per 1.03.03.

5.3.title: GMP Policy
sections: All employees, contractors, and visitors at GG facilities.
Management enforces; everyone complies; FSQA audits monthly.
Personal hygiene per GG-TRN-201: handwashing, hairnets/beard nets, clean clothing, no jewelry except plain band, wounds covered with bandage + glove.
No eating, drinking, gum, candy, or tobacco outside designated areas; no glass in production.
Personal items in lockers; medications controlled.
Illness reporting before shift (vomiting, diarrhea, fever, jaundice).
Visitors/contractors sign GMP acknowledgment and are escorted.
Violations are corrected immediately and may lead to discipline (write-up system).
GMP training records; visitor logs; monthly GMP audit (1.04.01R-IA-GMP).

5.03.01.title: Storage Areas & Packaging Materials
sections: GMP requirements for storage areas and packaging materials.
All dry storage, cooler storage, and packaging material storage.
Warehouse/inventory leads maintain daily; FSQA audits.
Product and packaging stored off the floor on clean pallets, 18 in. from walls for inspection/pest access.
Packaging protected from contamination (covered/bagged); partial cases resealed.
Chemicals never stored above or beside product/packaging.
Damaged goods segregated to the hold area (5.03).
Storage inspections (monthly GMP audit); temperature logs.

5.03.05.title: Operational Practices — Storage Areas
sections: All storage areas at packing facilities.
Production manager ensures cleaning crew availability; FSQA verifies.
Storage areas kept clean, well-ventilated, and protected from condensation, sewage, dust, and chemicals.
Cleaning per the master sanitation schedule with adequate frequency to prevent accumulation.
Ledges/overheads included in cleaning; spillage cleaned immediately.
Stored materials inspected on rotation for damage, pests, and dating.

5.03.06.title: Stock Rotation Procedure
sections: Stock rotation (FIFO/FEFO) for raw materials, packaging, WIP, and finished goods.
Inventory manager oversees; pickers follow FIFO; FSQA spot-checks.
All materials date-coded at receipt; finished goods carry pack date/TLC.
First-Expired-First-Out picking; NetSuite locations maintained so oldest stock is presented first (GG-TRN-207).
Monthly review for short-dated/expired stock; expired material moved to hold for disposition.
Cycle count/rotation reviews; hold log entries.

5.04.05.title: Product Operational Practices
sections: All packing lines and product handling.
Production manager; line leads enforce; FSQA audits.
Rework: product from end-of-line may be repacked if clean and in spec; floor product is discarded — never reworked.
Lines cleared and cleaned at changeover; allergen/organic sequencing where applicable.
Open product protected from overhead contamination; no work over exposed product during maintenance.
Totes/utensils food-grade, clean, and never used for chemicals or trash.
Packaging unwrapped only as needed; partial cases protected.
Product dropped or contaminated is discarded and the event noted.

5.04.08.title: Foreign Material Control
sections: All production, storage, and handling areas.
FSQA owns the program; all employees prevent and report.

Control sources: glass & brittle plastic per 5.09.02 register; wood (pallet control 5.09.16); metal (knife/blade control with issue log and intact-return verification); loose hardware via maintenance practices.
No glass, wood, or unnecessary brittle items in product zones; eyeglasses reported if damaged.
Maintenance after-work check: tools, fasteners, and parts reconciled; area released by QA before restart.
Foreign material findings in product trigger hold (5.03), deviation with RCA/CAPA, and customer notification assessment.
Breakage response: stop, contain, discard exposed product, clean, document.
Blade/knife logs; breakage reports; deviations and CAPA.

5.04.09-CL-R-03.title: Chemical Mixing Record (Sanitizer)
sections: Record of sanitizer mixing and concentration verification.
Chemical and target concentration (e.g., SparCHLOR 150 ppm)
Corrective action if out of range
Verify at each mixing and at intervals during use; out-of-range = re-mix and recheck before use.

5.04.11.title: Hand Washing
sections: All employees, contractors, and visitors at GG facilities.
Supervisors monitor compliance; sanitation stocks stations.
Wash hands: before starting work and entering production, after breaks, restroom, eating, touching face/hair, handling trash/chemicals, and whenever soiled.
Method: wet, soap, scrub 20 seconds including nails, rinse, dry with single-use towel, use towel on door where applicable.
Stations stocked with warm water, soap, towels; checked on pre-op; signage posted bilingual.
Glove use does not replace handwashing; wash before gloving.

5.06.01.title: Equipment Design
sections: Equipment design and condition requirements (sanitary design).
Maintenance maintains; FSQA approves new/modified equipment.
Food-contact surfaces smooth, non-toxic, cleanable, corrosion-resistant; no flaking paint or rust in product zones.
No hollow rollers/frames open to harborage where avoidable; caulking food-grade and intact.
Temporary repairs (tape, wire) prohibited in product zones; if unavoidable, documented and time-limited with QA approval.
New equipment reviewed for cleanability before commissioning; lubricants food-grade (H1) above product zones.
Equipment review records; maintenance work orders.

5.07.01.title: Cleaning and Sanitation
sections: Cleaning and sanitation program for packing facilities.
All production areas, equipment, and utensils.
Sanitation lead executes; QA verifies via pre-op and ATP.
Master cleaning schedule defines item, method, chemical, frequency, and responsibility for every area/equipment.
Daily: production lines, food-contact surfaces, floors, drains (dedicated tools, color-coded).
Periodic: overheads, walls, coolers, condensate management per schedule.
Seven-step wet cleaning where applicable: dry pick-up, pre-rinse, detergent scrub, rinse, inspect, sanitize (150 ppm SparCHLOR per 5.07.01.1), air dry.
Cleaning tools maintained, color-coded by zone, stored off the floor.
Master cleaning schedule sign-offs; sanitizer records; ATP logs.

5.07.01.1.title: Light Sanitizing SparCHLOR 150ppm
sections: Light sanitizing procedure using SparCHLOR at 150 ppm.
Food-contact surfaces and utensils between deep cleans.
Mix SparCHLOR to 150 ppm per label; verify with test strip; record on 5.04.09 form.
Apply to cleaned surfaces; contact time per label; air dry — no rinse at sanitizing strength.
PPE per SDS: gloves and eye protection when mixing.

5.07.01.1T.title: SparCHLOR Sanitizing Training (Bilingual)
sections: Bilingual training deck for SparCHLOR sanitizing (150/200 ppm).
What SparCHLOR is and approved uses
Mixing steps and verification with strips
Delivered at hire for sanitation roles and on procedure changes; sign-in kept with training records.

5.09.01.title: Buildings and Grounds
sections: Buildings and grounds maintenance for food safety.
All GG buildings, roofs, and exterior grounds.
Maintenance maintains structures; site managers inspect grounds.
Exterior: graded to drain, no standing water; vegetation trimmed 18 in. from walls; no pallet/equipment storage against the building; trash areas clean with closed lids.
Building shell: doors close tight with no daylight gaps, screens intact, wall/roof penetrations sealed.
Interior: floors/walls/ceilings cleanable and in repair; lights shielded/shatterproof (5.09.02); condensate controlled.
Monthly inspection via the GMP internal audit; deficiencies to maintenance work orders.

5.09.02.title: Glass and Brittle Materials Policy
sections: Glass and brittle plastic materials policy.
All GG facilities — production, storage, and adjacent areas.
FSQA owns the registers; department leads inspect; everyone reports breakage.
No unnecessary glass/brittle items in production; lights shielded or shatterproof.
Site registers (5.09.02-01R series) list every glass/brittle item with location and condition; inspected at the defined frequency (monthly).
Breakage response: stop work in the area, contain and discard any exposed product (25-ft rule or as assessed), clean with dedicated tools, inspect footwear, document, and re-inspect before restart.
Eyeglasses lost or broken in production are reported immediately.
Registers and inspection records; breakage reports.

5.09.02-01R-GH.title: Glass & Brittle Plastic Inspection (Greenhouse)
sections: Glass & brittle plastic register and inspection — Greenhouse.
Each item listed: location, description, type (glass/hard plastic/ceramic), shielded (Y/N)
Monthly condition check: intact / damaged / missing
Date, inspector, corrective action for damaged items
Inspect monthly and after any storm/maintenance work; update when items are added or removed.

5.09.02-01R.title: Glass & Brittle Plastic Inspection (Packing)
sections: Glass & brittle plastic register and inspection — Packing facility.
Itemized register by room (processing, coolers, dry storage, break rooms, offices adjacent to production)
Inspector signature and date; corrective actions

5.09.02-01R-PH.title: Glass & Brittle Plastic Inspection (PH)
sections: Glass & brittle plastic register and inspection — Packinghouse (PH site).
Same format as 5.09.02-01R for the PH location
Maintained by the PH FSQA designee.

5.09.16.title: Pallet Control
sections: Pallet control to prevent foreign material and contamination.
All pallets used for incoming goods, storage, and shipping.
Receiving inspects on arrival; warehouse rejects damaged pallets.
Accept only clean, dry, odor-free pallets in good repair.
Reject: broken boards, protruding nails, chemical/oil stains, pest evidence, severe mold — rejected pallets segregated and recorded.
Wood pallets kept off direct product contact: slip sheets or food-grade pallets for exposed product.

5.11.02.title: Chemical Inventory
sections: FSQA Supervisor verifies weekly inventory; maintenance performs counts.
Chemical inventory form lists each chemical, location, container count/quantity, and SDS reference.
Weekly count reconciles usage; discrepancies investigated (food defense relevance).
Only listed chemicals on site; new chemicals approved per 5.01.01 before arrival.
Monthly completion verified by FSQA Manager; deviations get CAPA.

5.13.01.title: Incoming Goods Inspection
sections: All deliveries of raw product, packaging, and materials.
Receiving staff inspect and record; QA notified of failures.
Before unloading: trailer condition (clean, dry, odor-free, no pests), seal verified against documents where applicable, temperature checked for refrigerated loads.
Goods inspected: condition, dating, lot legibility, spec conformity, allergen/organic segregation.
Receiving Log (5.13.01-R) completed for every receipt with lot capture for traceability.
Failures: reject or hold (5.03), record against supplier (1.06-01), notify QA.
NetSuite receipt entered in real time (GG-TRN-207).

5.13.01-R.title: Receiving Log
sections: One line per receipt; failures require QA notification and supplier record.

5.13.08.title: Operations Inspections — Pre-Op
sections: All food-contact surfaces visually clean (belts, tables, knives, totes)
Equipment reassembled correctly; no missing parts/fasteners
No cleaning tools/chemicals left in area
Completed every production day before start-up; failures re-cleaned and re-checked before release.

5.14.01.title: Preventive Maintenance & Repairs
sections: Preventive maintenance and repairs (food safety requirements).
All equipment and building systems affecting product safety.
Maintenance manager schedules PM (11.7 master schedule); QA releases areas after work.
PM scheduled per the master schedule based on manufacturer guidance and history.
Food-safe work rules: tools accounted for, food-grade (H1) lubricants in product zones, no work over exposed product, temporary repairs controlled per 5.06.01.
After work in production areas: clean-up, parts/fastener reconciliation, and QA release before restart.
Breakdown repairs in production documented with the same release.
Work orders retained with completion records.
PM schedule and completions; work orders with QA release.

5.14.01-03.title: Maintenance and PM
sections: Maintenance and PM execution detail (work order flow).
All maintenance work at GG facilities.
Work orders opened in the maintenance dashboard with priority and food-safety flag.
Parts staged outside production where possible; contamination risks assessed before work.
Completion requires checklist: debris removed, fasteners counted, guards reinstalled, lubricant type recorded, QA release for product zones.

5.14.08-02.title: Heavy Cleaning SparCHLOR 200ppm
sections: Heavy cleaning procedure using SparCHLOR at 200 ppm.
Deep cleaning of equipment and structural surfaces.
Used for periodic deep cleans and after contamination events.
Mix to 200 ppm, verify with strips, record; apply with required contact time; rinse food-contact surfaces after heavy-strength application per label.
Follow with pre-op verification before production.

5.14.12.title: ATP Monitoring
sections: Food-contact surfaces at all packing facilities.
QA performs swabs; FSQA trends results.
Swab sites rotate from the site list so all food-contact surfaces are covered over the cycle.
Pass/caution/fail RLU limits defined per device; fails are re-cleaned and re-swabbed until pass.
Results logged with site, RLU, action; trends reviewed monthly; repeat failures trigger CAPA and sanitation review.

5.14.13-01R-GH.title: Glass & Brittle Plastic Inspection (Greenhouse — Maintenance copy)
sections: Glass & brittle plastic inspection — Greenhouse (maintenance copy).
Same register/inspection as 5.09.02-01R-GH, maintained with the maintenance PM records
Cross-reference: completes the maintenance module requirement.

5.16.01.title: Environmental and Microbiological Monitoring
sections: Production environment (zones 2-4) and water at packing facilities.
FSQA defines sites/schedule; accredited lab analyzes; FSQA manages responses.
Risk-based swab map: zones 2 and 3 sites near product contact, drains, coolers; frequency per schedule.
Indicator organisms (APC, coliform, Listeria spp. environmental) per program; pathogens use approved methods.
Positive/over-limit results: intensified cleaning, vector swabbing, hold assessment for any product impact, deviation + RCA + CAPA.
Trending reviewed monthly and at management review.
Sampling schedule and map; lab results; corrective action records.

5.16.01-SA.title: Environmental Monitoring Procedure & Schedule (SA)
sections: Environmental monitoring procedure and schedule — SA site.
Site QA executes per the SA schedule; FSQA reviews.
Site-specific swab map and frequency per the SA schedule appendix.

5.16.01-W.title: Water Testing — Microbiological
sections: All water used in production, postharvest, and handwashing at GG sites.
FSQA schedules sampling; accredited labs analyze.
Sample points and frequency defined per site water map (municipal: annually + on events; well: quarterly generic E. coli; hydroponic systems per 2.09-4.09).
Acceptance criteria: potable standards for contact water; generic E. coli criteria for production water per FSMA.
Failures: stop use of source, assess product, treat/correct, retest before return — deviation + CAPA.
Results filed with the water system records.
Water test results; corrective actions; water system map.

5.16.10.title: Verification Schedule, Validation & Effectiveness
sections: Verification schedule, validation, and effectiveness checks.
All food safety program verification activities.
FSQA Manager owns the verification master schedule.

Master verification schedule lists every verification: pre-op, ATP, internal audits, water testing, environmental monitoring, calibration, mock recall, plan reviews — with frequency and responsibility.
Validation: control measures (sanitizer concentrations, CCP limits) validated against scientific/regulatory references at adoption and on change.
Effectiveness: CAPA effectiveness checks per 1.03.03; trend reviews at management review.
Verification master schedule; validation file; review minutes.

5.17.04.title: Outbound Truck/Trailer Inspection Log
sections: Inspect every outbound trailer before loading; failures: reject trailer and note carrier.

6.01.01.title: HACCP Plan with Flow Chart and Hazard Analysis
sections: HACCP plan with flow chart and hazard analysis.
HACCP/PC team led by the PCQI/FSQA Manager; trained monitors at CCPs.
Product description, intended use, and flow diagram maintained and verified on-site annually.
Hazard analysis for each step (biological, chemical incl. allergens where applicable, physical) with severity/likelihood justification.
CCPs/preventive controls identified (e.g., wash water sanitizer, metal detection where installed, cooling) with validated critical limits.
Monitoring procedures/frequency and trained monitors defined; real-time records (no pre/back-filling).
Deviations: hold product, correct, RCA + CAPA per 1.03.03 and GG-TRN-202 rules.
Verification per 5.16.10; plan reassessed annually and on change.
HACCP plan and hazard analysis; CCP monitoring records; verification records.

7.02.02-PC-01.title: Preventive Controls Policy
sections: Preventive Controls policy (FSMA PC for Human Food).
All GG facilities subject to 21 CFR Part 117.
A qualified PCQI oversees the food safety plan at each facility; individuals must be qualified for their PC duties.
Preventive controls identified from the hazard analysis: process, allergen (labeling/segregation), sanitation, supply-chain (1.06-01 + FSVP), and the recall plan (1.07-RT-01).
Each PC has monitoring, corrective action, and verification defined in the food safety plan.
Training: PC-relevant employees trained; PCQI certificate(s) on file (FSPCA — see training matrix).
Reanalysis at least every 3 years and on change/new hazard information.
Food safety plan; PCQI certificates; monitoring and verification records.

11.1-PM-01.title: Preventive Maintenance Policy (Orlando)
sections: All equipment, machinery, and systems at the Orlando site.
Maintenance department implements; equipment operators report anomalies.
Scheduled maintenance per manufacturer recommendations, industry standards, and history; schedule reviewed/updated annually.
Records of all PM retained; compliance tracked on the master schedule (11.7).

11.7.title: Master Preventive Maintenance (GG MCG)
sections: Master workbook tracking PM compliance across assets; reviewed monthly; ties to 5.14.01.

1.08-FD-EP-IN.title: Emergency Preparedness (Indiana)
sections: Emergency preparedness plan for the Indiana facility.
All emergencies affecting food safety or business continuity at the IN site: fire, severe weather/tornado, power loss, water loss, refrigeration failure, security events.
Site manager is emergency coordinator; FSQA assesses product impact.
Emergency contacts and utility shutoffs are posted; employees trained at hire.
Tornado: move to designated shelter areas; account for all personnel.
Power/refrigeration failure: record cooler temperatures hourly; product time/temperature assessed by QA before release.
Water loss or boil notice: stop production; assess product contact water use before restart.
After any event, inspect the facility (GMP walkthrough) and document product disposition before resuming.
Drill records; event reports; product disposition records.
